title: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, Upsert, and Mutation Evidence · Query Trace: Delete Scope
summary: A ticket import and reassignment workflow must change only intended rows and retain evidence of what happened. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql delete scope increment in a guarded ticket-mutation workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish row deletion, truncation, and retention-safe archival while predicting referential effects.
Preview an UPDATE predicate as a SELECT and verify changed-row count and postconditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.
